content: I will take a look at it today

author: jtraglia
content: Might not be worth looking into if we decide to include 8282 in glamsterdam. It would solve these issues. I think we’re going to talk about this at ACDT today.

author: stefanbratanov

author: pk910
content:
<@592004585506340885> can we make the dequeue rate dynamic, so it reads the number of elements to dequeue via the system call calldata?
That would allow us to easily adjust the dequeue rate at a later fork if needed,  and shouldn't be hard to add (replace the constant by a calldataload / number conversion)

author: jtraglia
content: This would be a change in the system contract?

author: pk910
content: (context: new builder deposit contract)

author: pk910
content: yes

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm not something I can answer/change. <@534934855113506836> is the author of that.

author: jtraglia
content: Also not sure of the complexity of this. Would need to ask an EL person.

author: pk910
content:
Yea, some extra complexity to call the system contract with some calldata,  but I think that'd be trivial.
Guess the biggest blocker is the customization of the ASM code (even if trivial), as we can't say it's equal to the other queue contracts then 😄

author: jtraglia
content: If we wanted to change it in the future, wouldn't it be easier to just deploy a new contract with the changed number? 😅

author: pk910
content: I think deploying a new contract for an existing workflow should be the last option we ever take.   that's a big disruption and will cause lost funds as the old contract is hardcoded in other contracts & toolings

author: jtraglia
content: Ah true.

author: stefanbratanov
content: Just to confirm cause it was at the end of a long call, we agreed to implement that in clients but not have a specs release yet?

author: jtraglia
content: Yes. That is correct. Will merge the specs PR after clients have implemented & approved the PR.

author: jtraglia
content: And I wouldn't make a release until Thursday anyway. After we've discussed it more at ACDE.

author: stefanbratanov
content: Ok, will try to implement this before ACDD

author: jtraglia
content: Ah great, _another_ ACD call 😅 (jk)

author: stefanbratanov
content: All Core Devs Debugging!

author: nflaig
content: so it seems reasonable to me to keep builders at the fork logic as is in the current spec PR to avoid (1) having some entities use the unstaked builder flow to bring builder payloads onchain right after the fork or (2) to only have local payloads for 2-3 epochs after the fork which means apps that rely on private order flow (eg. cow swap) would not work, from [Justin's list](https://github.com/jtraglia/kurtosis-devnets/tree/master/gloas-deposits) this leaves us with s2, s3 and s5, which all rely on pre-validating deposits in the `pending_deposit` queue which clients can start doing few epochs ahead of the fork (I believe prysm already does this) and these caches/optimization can be removed as soon as gloas is live on mainnet
